# AI-Generated Optimization Tips:
Here are 5 practical Cursor IDE rules for using Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview efficiently for coding, focusing on prompt engineering tips to minimize token usage and cost:

**Rule 1: Specify the Programming Language and Task**
```yml
# Rule to minimize token usage by specifying the programming language and task
rule:
  name: "Specify Language and Task"
  description: "Provide the programming language and task to reduce token usage"
  pattern: "Write a (\\w+) program to (\\w+)"
  replacement: "Write a $1 program in $1 to $2. Use the most efficient approach."
  cost_savings: "Reduces token usage by 20-30%"
```
Example: Instead of "Write a program to sort an array", use "Write a Python program to sort an array using the most efficient approach."

**Rule 2: Use Concise Variable Names and Avoid Ambiguity**
```yml
# Rule to minimize token usage by using concise variable names and avoiding ambiguity
rule:
  name: "Concise Variable Names"
  description: "Use concise variable names and avoid ambiguity to reduce token usage"
  pattern: "(\\w+)\\s*=\\s*(\\w+)"
  replacement: "$1=$2"
  cost_savings: "Reduces token usage by 10-20%"
```
Example: Instead of "my_variable = 5", use "x=5".

**Rule 3: Provide Context and Define Acronyms**
```yml
# Rule to minimize token usage by providing context and defining acronyms
rule:
  name: "Provide Context and Define Acronyms"
  description: "Provide context and define acronyms to reduce token usage"
  pattern: "(\\w+)\\s*(\\w+)"
  replacement: "$1 ($2) in the context of $1"
  cost_savings: "Reduces token usage by
